title: dasyphyllum brasiliense var. barnadesioides
intro: Dasyphyllum brasiliense var. barnadesioides, also known as "mora de muerto" or "black brush," is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, native to South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dasyphyllum brasiliense var. barnadesioides include potential uses in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and bronchodilator properties. Herbal preparations of Dasyphyllum brasiliense var. barnadesioides can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to promote respiratory health and well-being.
